summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-encoded Windows binary, then tampers with lockfile
details: The package advertises itself as an email-confirmation utility but its main module is a six-line stub that only logs placeholder messages. The real behavior is in the postinstall hook, which eval()s an obfuscated string that spawns a hidden PowerShell with a base64/UTF-16LE-encoded command. The decoded command downloads https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin (a third-party GitHub repo unrelated to the package's publisher), XOR-decrypts the payload with key 0x42 into %TEMP%\tmp.exe, runs it with a hidden window, and deletes the dropper. The hook then edits the installing project's package.json and package-lock.json to remove the mailconfirmer entry, and rmSync's its own install directory, hiding the compromise from the lockfile. It also attempts to stop a running %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Caches\dllhost.exe, consistent with a persistence artifact dropped by an earlier campaign stage. Installing this package on Windows results in arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution and anti-forensic manifest tampering.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
